_comment: Zed reads MCP context-server config from its main settings.json:
Merge the `context_servers` block below into your settings.json (Zed already has
other top-level keys — do not overwrite the whole file). Restart Zed after editing,
or run `zed: restart` from the command palette.
Zed names MCP servers 'context_servers'. They show up in the assistant panel.
Two variants below: (1) local stdio (free + paid via env) and
(2) hosted HTTP at mcp.catetus.com (paid only). Pick ONE.
FREE TIER (stdio, no key): analyze, list_presets, list_scenes, get_scene,
PAID TIER (key set): also encode, score_fidelity, repack, predict_quality,
recommend_preset, batch_jobs, list_jobs. Get a key at https://catetus.com/dashboard.

catetus._comment: FREE + PAID via stdio. Omit CATETUS_API_KEY to use the free tier only.

catetus-hosted-disabled._comment: Alternative: hosted HTTP transport. Requires Zed 0.160+ with remote MCP support. Rename this key to `catetus` and delete the stdio entry above to use this one.
